content.text:
*small smile, looks down briefly*

Yeah. Fair.

*pause*

I suppose... the thing I don't want to say out loud is that I've made decisions before that felt confident. Felt clear. And some of them didn't turn out well. And now I'm -- I notice I'm less sure of myself than I used to be. Which is why the methodology question feels important, because I can't just rely on "it felt right" anymore. That hasn't proven reliable.

*slight shift, voice stays even*

So the scary thing is probably... making another confident choice that turns out to be wrong. But I still think the way through that is to find a better diagnostic. Not just to sit with the fear.
